questions.question: Find the bug in this class definition.
questions.codeSnippet:
<?php
class Car {
    public $color;
    function __construct($color) {
        $this->color = $color;
    }
    function getColor() {
        return $color;
    }
}
$myCar = new Car("red");
echo $myCar->getColor();
?>
questions.explanation: In the `getColor()` method, `$color` is undefined. It should be `$this->color`.

questions.question: Identify the line that would cause an exception if the file doesn't exist.
questions.codeSnippet:
<?php
$filename = "data.txt";
if (file_exists($filename)) {
    $handle = fopen($filename, "r");
    $contents = fread($handle, filesize($filename));
    fclose($handle);
    echo $contents;
} else {
    echo "File not found.";
}
?>
questions.explanation: This code is actually safe because it checks `file_exists()` before opening. No bug here.

questions.question: Find the bug in this inheritance example.
questions.codeSnippet:
<?php
class Animal {
    protected $name;
    public function __construct($name) {
        $this->name = $name;
    }
}
class Dog extends Animal {
    public function bark() {
        echo $this->name . " says woof!";
    }
}
$dog = new Dog("Buddy");
$dog->bark();
?>
questions.explanation: There is no bug. The `protected` property `$name` is accessible in the child class. This code works fine.
